nativeRange.habitat: Hard substrate (rock, dead coral skeleton, gorgonian skeletons) in the photic zone
nativeRange.biotope: Coral reef and rocky reef, often found growing over dead gorgonian skeletons and similar calcareous or fibrous substrates

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that graze macroalgae (tangs, rabbitfish at high stocking densities)

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Gorgonian-Encrusting Cryptonemia crenulata.

summary: Gorgonian Encrusting is a thin, frilly red macroalga (Cryptonemia crenulata, family Halymeniaceae) that spreads as an encrusting sheet over dead gorgonian skeletons, bare rock, and coral rubble. It grows slowly and is kept primarily as a decorative display specimen in reef aquariums under medium lighting.
careNotes: Cryptonemia crenulata is a sheet-like red macroalga that spreads as a thin, frilly encrusting growth over hard substrates — dead gorgonian skeletons, bare rock, and coral rubble are all commonly documented substrates in the wild and in captivity. The trade name 'Gorgonian Encrusting' reflects this growth habit rather than any taxonomic relationship to gorgonian corals. In aquaria the species tends to grow slowly and is generally kept as a display specimen rather than a nutrient-export workhorse. Standard reef water parameters are generally considered adequate: temperature around 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity around SG 1.023–1.026. Medium-intensity full-spectrum lighting is typically advised; overly blue-heavy coral lighting has been noted as suboptimal for many red macroalgae. Micronutrient supplementation (particularly iron and iodine) may support coloration and growth rate. Hobby documentation on this species is limited, and husbandry guidance is largely drawn from family-level care experience with related Halymeniaceae species. Propagation in captivity is by fragmentation — cutting or splitting encrusted fragments.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Provides species description and genus context; specific husbandry parameters extrapolated from genus and family care guidance.

additional.name: World Register of Marine Species (WoRMS)
additional.notes: Confirmed accepted name Cryptonemia crenulata (J.Agardh) J.Agardh, 1851; taxonomy: Order Halymeniales, Family Halymeniaceae.

additional.name: GulfBase
additional.notes: Confirmed wide Atlantic/Indo-Pacific distribution and hard-substrate habitat; depth range 10–20 m.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — General Macroalgae Care
additional.notes: General reef macroalgae parameters: temp 72–78°F (22–26°C), pH 8.1–8.4, nitrate 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm; used as baseline for this species.
